title: Generic AI aesthetic
review_instruction: Flag when the UI relies on stock AI-dashboard tropes instead of product-specific design decisions.
observable_signals: Interchangeable SaaS look; generic premium tone; no domain-specific structure, copy, or visual language

title: Generic dark-SaaS shell
review_instruction: Flag when the screen defaults to a blue-black or gray-black 'premium dashboard' look without a product reason.
observable_signals: Dark gradient canvas; cyan/purple accents; detached floating surfaces; style feels imported rather than owned

title: Premium-by-decoration chrome
review_instruction: Flag when polish is coming from decorative effects rather than strong layout, hierarchy, and content choices.
observable_signals: Glassmorphism; glows; blur haze; frosted panels; gradient borders; dramatic shadows

title: Excessive roundness / pill overload
review_instruction: Flag when large radii or pill shapes are used across too many components, making the interface soft and toy-like.
observable_signals: Pill buttons, tabs, badges, chips, and cards everywhere; 20px+ radii used as a default

title: Template typography / weak type hierarchy
review_instruction: Flag when typography feels unbranded or the hierarchy is weak, with headings, labels, body, and metadata too similar.
observable_signals: Default-stack feel; unclear heading structure; weak contrast between heading/body/label roles

title: Weak color judgment / low contrast
review_instruction: Flag when the palette hurts readability or feels arbitrary rather than intentional and calm.
observable_signals: Low-contrast text; muddy surfaces; accents that fight; muted gray-blue text that is hard to scan

title: Ornamental microcopy / fake product voice
review_instruction: Flag decorative copy that sounds clever or premium but does not help the user complete a task.
observable_signals: Taglines, slogans, explanatory filler, or faux-poetic copy that could be removed without losing function

title: Eyebrow-label / uppercase-metadata habit
review_instruction: Flag tiny uppercase labels, letter-spaced metadata, or mini-notes used as decoration rather than structure.
observable_signals: Small uppercase pre-headings; muted section notes; decorative metadata above otherwise simple content

title: Low distinctiveness / template feel
review_instruction: Flag screens that could belong to almost any generic SaaS product because the design lacks domain-specific choices.
observable_signals: Familiar dashboard composition; generic labels; no product-specific information architecture or character

title: Gimmicky hover / micro-motion
review_instruction: Flag motion that adds flair but not clarity, especially default translate, scale, or bounce behaviors.
observable_signals: Hover translateX/Y; scale-on-hover; motion used everywhere instead of only where feedback is needed

title: Card/panel nesting and wrapper bloat
review_instruction: Flag unnecessary container layers that fragment content and make the layout feel overbuilt.
observable_signals: Cards inside cards; panels inside panels; many shells that add chrome but not structure

title: Weak spacing, alignment, hierarchy, and positioning
review_instruction: Flag inconsistent spacing or placement that makes the screen look off-grid, uneven, or accidentally composed.
observable_signals: Misaligned baselines; uneven gaps; overlapping elements; headings and content not sharing a grid

title: Wasted space / density problems
review_instruction: Flag oversized padding, large empty regions, or sparse composition that reduces clarity rather than improving it.
observable_signals: Huge margins; dead zones; overpadded sections; low information density with no readability gain

title: Detached dashboard-shell cliché
review_instruction: Flag the stock composition of floating rounded sidebar plus detached main shell when it is not driven by the product.
observable_signals: Floating sidebar shell; brand block at top; detached content card; ornamental outer framing

title: Hero-inside-dashboard mistake
review_instruction: Flag marketing-style hero sections or decorative header bands inside internal tools or dense product views.
observable_signals: Large welcome banner; decorative hero copy; oversized dashboard header taking space from real work

title: Invented layouts instead of product-appropriate structures
review_instruction: Flag layouts that are visually clever but worse for scanning, navigation, or task completion than a standard product structure.
observable_signals: Asymmetric composition with no payoff; unusual section ordering; structure feels invented for style

title: Metric-card / KPI-grid-first instinct
review_instruction: Flag top-row KPI cards used as filler or ceremony when the primary user task is not metric monitoring.
observable_signals: Three- or four-card stat strip; generic numbers; metrics that do not drive the screen's main job

title: Filler side-panels and rails
review_instruction: Flag side panels or rails that mostly exist to fill space instead of supporting a real workflow.
observable_signals: Today rails; recent activity blocks; quota widgets; side CTA panels with weak task value

title: Mixed alignment logic
review_instruction: Flag screens where some blocks follow a strict grid while others float center-ish or use unrelated alignment rules.
observable_signals: Left-aligned content mixed with centered shells; inconsistent column starts; wandering edges

title: Breakpoint fragility / mobile collapse
review_instruction: Across widths, flag layouts that collapse into a long stack, lose grouping, overflow, or become hard to operate.
observable_signals: Single-column beige sandwich; broken grouping; clipped content; tiny tap targets; horizontal overflow

title: Reference fidelity
review_instruction: When a screenshot, Figma frame, or designer reference is provided, compare against it and flag drift toward generic Codex UI.
observable_signals: Changed composition; spacing drift; wrong typography; token drift; different components; genericized styling

title: Design-system / token compliance
review_instruction: Flag components that ignore the product's spacing scale, radii, typography, border treatment, elevation, or color tokens.
observable_signals: Random radii; new hex codes; inconsistent borders; mismatched type scale; components that do not belong

title: Same geometry reused everywhere
review_instruction: Flag interfaces where every major surface and control shares the same shell treatment, creating sameness.
observable_signals: Same radius, padding, and border treatment on sidebar, cards, buttons, panels, and modals

title: Fake analytics / filler data viz
review_instruction: Flag charts, donut visuals, progress bars, or percentages that look decorative or disconnected from a real task.
observable_signals: Hand-wavy percentages; unlabeled charts; generic pipeline bars; visual metrics that exist mainly to fill space

title: Badge/tag/status-dot overproduction
review_instruction: Flag screens that wrap too many statuses, rows, or nav items in chips, badges, counts, or colored dots.
observable_signals: Tag on every row; nav count badges everywhere; live dots; status chips creating visual noise

title: Wrapper / panel-style proliferation
review_instruction: Flag screens that use too many near-identical surface styles instead of a small, coherent set of containers.
observable_signals: Panel, rail-panel, table-panel, shell, workspace block, and other one-off surface treatments on one screen

title: Overdecorated controls instead of plain functional primitives
review_instruction: Flag controls that are stylized beyond their functional role when a simpler primitive would be clearer.
observable_signals: Fancy tabs; animated inputs; decorative icon backgrounds; ornamental dropdowns; stylized controls for no task reason

title: Theme integrity / dark-mode legibility
review_instruction: Flag theme issues that break readability or consistency, especially in dark mode or across surface levels.
observable_signals: Unreadable text; muddy backgrounds; invisible borders; washed-out accents; inconsistent surface contrast

title: Local inconsistency / change spillover
review_instruction: Flag individual sections or components that look edited in isolation and no longer match the surrounding UI language.
observable_signals: One card or form uses a different radius, spacing, type scale, shadow, or interaction pattern than the rest

title: Placeholder or faux-product content
review_instruction: Flag labels and content that feel fabricated, ornamental, or disconnected from a plausible product vocabulary.
observable_signals: Live pulse; operator checklist; night shift; team focus; decorative labels that do not map to real product concepts

title: Obvious visual defects left unreviewed
review_instruction: Flag visible defects that a basic UI review should have caught before shipping.
observable_signals: Clipped text; overflow; broken corner joins; inconsistent radii; spacing glitches; unfinished-looking edges

title: Missing component states / incomplete interaction design
review_instruction: Flag components that appear to have only a default state, with little evidence of considered interaction design.
observable_signals: Missing or inconsistent hover, focus, selected, disabled, loading, empty, or error states

title: Task clarity hidden behind decoration
review_instruction: Flag screens where decorative structure or style makes the primary task harder to find, scan, or execute.
observable_signals: Primary action buried; important data secondary to chrome; ornamental sections competing with functional content
